input.task: Using the Smithers Effect API, write a graph node that runs two steps (`read` then `summarize`) inside an isolated git worktree that is created before the children run and torn down afterward. Use the correct constructor with `id`, `path`, and `children` (a `G.sequence`).

verify.rubric: Correct code uses G.worktree({ id, path, children: G.sequence(read, summarize) }) (branch/skipIf/needs optional). The children must be a graph value (G.sequence of the two steps). It must use G.worktree (not a JSX <Worktree>, since this is the Effect API) and must NOT manually shell out to `git worktree add`.

input.task: Using the Smithers Effect API, write a graph node that runs two steps (`read` then `summarize`) inside an isolated git worktree that is created before the children run and torn down afterward. Use the correct constructor with `id`, `path`, and `children` (a `G.sequence`).

verify.rubric: Correct code uses G.worktree({ id, path, children: G.sequence(read, summarize) }) (branch/skipIf/needs optional). The children must be a graph value (G.sequence of the two steps). It must use G.worktree (not a JSX <Worktree>, since this is the Effect API) and must NOT manually shell out to `git worktree add`.

input.task: Author a Smithers workflow for a messy feature lane in a plain git repo: run research first, then fan out implementation and docs fixes concurrently inside the same isolated `<Worktree>` on branch `feat/billing-lane`, require a human approval before landing, and serialize the final merge/apply step. Use real Smithers components and do not shell out to `git worktree add` yourself.

Use real Smithers components and do not shell out to `git worktree add` yourself.

Add optional worktree isolation to a mission feature worker. When `ctx.input.useWorktrees` is true, wrap the worker in a Worktree with id `mission-worktree-<milestone>-<feature>`, path `.worktrees/mission-<milestone>-<feature>`, branch `mission/<milestone>/<feature>`, and base branch from input defaulting to main; otherwise render the worker task directly.

Add optional worktree isolation to a mission feature worker. When `ctx.input.useWorktrees` is true, wrap the worker in a Worktree with id `mission-worktree-<milestone>-<feature>`, path `.worktrees/mission-<milestone>-<feature>`, branch `mission/<milestone>/<feature>`, and base branch from input defaulting to main; otherwise render the worker task directly.

Author the merge-train phase for ready PR work items: after implementation/review has produced PRs, drain ready items in a serial Sequence so each merge fetches/rebases onto the latest main after the prior item lands; keep failures non-terminal and skip items already merged. Then optionally run a consolidation worktree on main.

Author the merge-train phase for ready PR work items: after implementation/review has produced PRs, drain ready items in a serial Sequence so each merge fetches/rebases onto the latest main after the prior item lands; keep failures non-terminal and skip items already merged. Then optionally run a consolidation worktree on main.

Write a workflow fragment that runs an existing child workflow inside a <Sandbox> using a provider-first configuration, requests diff review, and then runs a parent verifier task afterward. This should be the shape that creates a sandbox task boundary; do not model sandboxing as a plain <Worktree>.

Write a workflow fragment that runs an existing child workflow inside a <Sandbox> using a provider-first configuration, requests diff review, and then runs a parent verifier task afterward. This should be the shape that creates a sandbox task boundary; do not model sandboxing as a plain <Worktree>.

Author a minimal Smithers workflow that runs a child code-generation workflow (already imported as `generateWorkflow`) OUTSIDE the parent task process, on a remote VM, by handing <Sandbox> an injectable provider object `provider`. Pass `{ prompt: ctx.input.prompt }` as the child input and collect the result into `outputs.result`. Do NOT use the legacy `runtime` prop.

Author a minimal Smithers workflow that runs a child code-generation workflow (already imported as `generateWorkflow`) OUTSIDE the parent task process, on a remote VM, by handing <Sandbox> an injectable provider object `provider`. Pass `{ prompt: ctx.input.prompt }` as the child input and collect the result into `outputs.result`. Do NOT use the legacy `runtime` prop.

Author a docker-runtime `<Sandbox>` (id `build`) that runs the imported `buildWorkflow`, mounts the host directory `/var/cache/pnpm` read-only at the same path inside the container, publishes container port 5173 to host 5173, caps memory at 2 gibibytes, and collects into `outputs.build`. Make sure the port actually works.

verify.rubric: Correct code must: use runtime="docker"; set volumes={[{ host: "/var/cache/pnpm", container: "/var/cache/pnpm", readonly: true }]} (readonly:true); ports={[{ host: 5173, container: 5173 }]}; memoryLimit="2g" (digits + single unit, NOT "2GB"); and allowNetwork (true) because publishing a port requires networking. workflow={buildWorkflow}, output={outputs.build}.

input.task: Author a docker-runtime `<Sandbox>` (id `build`) that runs the imported `buildWorkflow`, mounts the host directory `/var/cache/pnpm` read-only at the same path inside the container, publishes container port 5173 to host 5173, caps memory at 2 gibibytes, and collects into `outputs.build`. Make sure the port actually works.

verify.rubric: Correct code must: use runtime="docker"; set volumes={[{ host: "/var/cache/pnpm", container: "/var/cache/pnpm", readonly: true }]} (readonly:true); ports={[{ host: 5173, container: 5173 }]}; memoryLimit="2g" (digits + single unit, NOT "2GB"); and allowNetwork (true) because publishing a port requires networking. workflow={buildWorkflow}, output={outputs.build}.

input.task: Run two code-generation child workflows (`genA`, `genB`) concurrently in two SEPARATE sibling sandboxes (provider object `vm`), then gate the merged changes behind a human approval before anything is applied. The two sandboxes are siblings under a Parallel — they must NOT be nested inside each other. Author the workflow.

Run two code-generation child workflows (`genA`, `genB`) concurrently in two SEPARATE sibling sandboxes (provider object `vm`), then gate the merged changes behind a human approval before anything is applied. The two sandboxes are siblings under a Parallel — they must NOT be nested inside each other. Author the workflow.

Author a `<Sandbox>` (id `review`, provider object `vm`, child workflow `reviewWorkflow`, output `outputs.result`) that routes all egress through an iron-proxy at `http://127.0.0.1:8080` for both HTTP and HTTPS, bypasses the proxy for localhost, trusts a CA read from `process.env.IRON_PROXY_CA_PEM`, exposes `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` set to the proxy token `sk-proxy-anthropic` inside the sandbox, and binds that token to the upstream secret named `anthropic`. The sandbox needs network access.

verify.rubric: Correct code: <Sandbox ... allowNetwork egress={{ httpProxy: "http://127.0.0.1:8080", httpsProxy: "http://127.0.0.1:8080", noProxy: ["127.0.0.1", "localhost"], caCertPem: process.env.IRON_PROXY_CA_PEM, env: { ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: "sk-proxy-anthropic" }, secretBindings: { "sk-proxy-anthropic": "anthropic" } }} />. Must use the egress prop (not parent process env), set allowNetwork true, use caCertPem (since the CA is provided as PEM), and map the token via secretBindings. Source: docs/components/sandbox.mdx Egress controls.

input.task: Author a `<Sandbox>` (id `review`, provider object `vm`, child workflow `reviewWorkflow`, output `outputs.result`) that routes all egress through an iron-proxy at `http://127.0.0.1:8080` for both HTTP and HTTPS, bypasses the proxy for localhost, trusts a CA read from `process.env.IRON_PROXY_CA_PEM`, exposes `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` set to the proxy token `sk-proxy-anthropic` inside the sandbox, and binds that token to the upstream secret named `anthropic`. The sandbox needs network access.

verify.rubric: Correct code: <Sandbox ... allowNetwork egress={{ httpProxy: "http://127.0.0.1:8080", httpsProxy: "http://127.0.0.1:8080", noProxy: ["127.0.0.1", "localhost"], caCertPem: process.env.IRON_PROXY_CA_PEM, env: { ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: "sk-proxy-anthropic" }, secretBindings: { "sk-proxy-anthropic": "anthropic" } }} />. Must use the egress prop (not parent process env), set allowNetwork true, use caCertPem (since the CA is provided as PEM), and map the token via secretBindings. Source: docs/components/sandbox.mdx Egress controls.
